title: Evaluation Datasets, Hard Negatives, Leakage, and Experiment Design · Language Clinic: Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual
summary: An evaluation set contains synthetic easy questions generated from the indexed chunks, uses the same examples to tune and test, and has no hard negatives, slices, or versioned judgments. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hard negative counterfactual increment in versioned retrieval and answer test collecti…
objectives: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.
Create relevance and claim-support judgments with rubrics, independent assessors, adjudication, agreement, and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-eval-datasets-judgments-3
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-eval-datasets-judgments-3.
# Case 5798: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def worked_rag_eval_datasets_judgments_3(queries: int = 120, assessors: int = 2, hard_negatives: int = 36, leaked_queries: int = 0, agreement: float = 0.78, slices_covered: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "query3dekrow",
        "tenant5798",
        "indexworked35798",
        "prompt3dekrow5798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if queries <= 0 or assessors < 1 or hard_negatives < 0:
        return False, queries, "dataset-shape"
    if leaked_queries > 0:
        return False, leaked_queries, "evaluation-leakage"
    if agreement < 0.0 or agreement > 1.0:
        return False, 0, "agreement-range"
    if assessors < 2 or slices_covered < 4:
        return False, slices_covered, "judgment-coverage"
    assert hard_negatives < queries
    return True, queries, "versioned-eval-set"

steps.title: RAG and Python Build Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual evidence · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counterfa…
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-hard-negative-counterfactual-zlqdvr" that uses case 1246 to build versioned judgments with hard negatives, slice coverage, agreement, and leakage control. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-hard-negative-counterfactual-zlqdvr
# Competency: rag-hard-negative-counterfactual.
# Case 1246: a public-benefits policy change.
# Operating constraint: keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_hard_negative_counterfactual_zlqdvr(queries: int = 120, assessors: int = 2, hard_negatives: int = 36, leaked_queries: int = 0, agreement: float = 0.78, slices_covered: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_zlqdvr = "zlqdvr-rvdqlz-1246"
    assert evidence_variant_zlqdvr
    evidence_axes_zlqdvr = (
        "corpuszlqdvr",
        "queryrvdqlz",
        "tenant1246",
        "indexzlqdvr1246",
        "promptrvdqlz1246",
        "evaluation1246zlqdvr",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_zlqdvr)) == 6
    if queries <= 0 or assessors < 1 or hard_negatives < 0:
        return False, queries, "dataset-shape"
    if leaked_queries > 0:
        return False, leaked_queries, "evaluation-leakage"
    if agreement < 0.0 or agreement > 1.0:
        return False, 0, "agreement-range"
    if assessors < 2 or slices_covered < 4:
        return False, slices_covered, "judgment-coverage"
    assert hard_negatives < queries
    return True, queries, "versioned-eval-set"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 2822 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build versioned judgments with hard negatives, slice coverage, agreement, and leakage control; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual model · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counter….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual model · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counter…, keep Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.

hints: For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual model · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counter…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Defend the Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual model · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counter…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 2822 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build versioned judgments with hard negatives, slice coverage, agreement, and leakage control; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include lexical, semantic, stale, conflicting, unauthorized, near-duplicate, adversarial, and counterfactual hard negatives.

For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Hard Negative Counterfactual model · Theory Rag Hard Negative Counter…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: Random irrelevant documents provide sufficient negative examples.
